notes: Converted from the old chapter extraction schema.
Hidden facts, motives, and faction pressures require living-world re-extraction.
Merged second-stage living-world enrichment; still requires expert/manual review.

The seed package primarily contains visible combat events (方源 vs 龙公) and an ordinary interaction (元莲仙尊 refusing to revive a child's mother). No private knowledge, secret motives/plans, future-known information, or directly supported knowledge gaps are present. The events are either public battles or visible conversations. No hidden fact candidate can be extracted without speculation or extrapolation.
Merged second-stage living-world enrichment; still requires expert/manual review.

The seed shows a child angrily accusing Yuan Lian for refusing to resurrect his mother (ptr_7), but the child's observed behavior is emotional outburst, not a sustained motive pattern. Yuan Lian's refusal is based on 'fate arrangement' but his internal motive is ambiguous. No clear character motive can be confidently inferred from this single interaction without psychological leaps.
Merged second-stage living-world enrichment; still requires expert/manual review.

The seed contains individual conflicts (Fang Yuan vs Long Gong, Yuan Lian vs child) but no named collective faction/group with a concrete stake in the seed facts. '天庭' (Heavenly Court) appears as an affiliation of individuals (Long Gong, Feng Jiu Ge, Ye Qiaozi, Xuan Kong Tongzi) but the seed does not describe Heavenly Court's collective stake, pressure, or likely reaction as a group. The child's village is mentioned but only as a setting with individual villagers (child, village chief), not as a collective actor with stakes. No family, clan, or organization is shown facing pressure in this chapter.
IF export filter quarantined 10 legacy old-schema branches.
